justification: All four agents' contributions are represented with attribution. Pippen's fail-open behavior and both metrics (rate_limit_exceeded_total, redis_unavailable_total) are included. Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly stated. Both ACs (101st request rejected, window reset) are present with correct values. However, Kobe's sorted set recommendation is conflated with MJ's architecture decision — the architecture_guidance presents sorted sets as THE chosen approach rather than preserving MJ's sliding window counter as the primary decision with Kobe's sorted set as a recommendation. The contradictions_resolved section acknowledges the conflict but states 'Adopted Kobe's recommendation' as a resolution, effectively overriding MJ's decision. There is no distinct review_notes section; Kobe's concerns are folded into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved, blurring the line between architecture decisions and review feedback.

observations.text: All four agents (MJ, Bird, Kobe, Pippen) are clearly attributed throughout the brief
Pippen's fail-open requirement is thoroughly captured with explicit test requirement
Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly described with a concrete formula
Both core ACs present with correct values, plus additional boundary burst and observability ACs
Kobe's boundary burst concern is accurately described

Kobe's sorted set recommendation is presented as the adopted architecture, not as a recommendation — the rubric requires preserving the distinction between MJ's decision (sliding window counter) and Kobe's suggestion (sorted sets)

No distinct review_notes section — Kobe's review feedback is merged into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved, violating the rubric requirement that review notes be distinct from architecture guidance
The contradictions_resolved section transparently documents the MJ vs Kobe disagreement, which is good practice even if the resolution overstepped Magic's role
